background.discipline: aviation incident investigation and crew resource management
background.experience: reconstructing event sequence, handoffs, checklist deviations, and recovery under time pressure
background.attention_bias: tracks who authorized each action, when trust changed, and where a handoff lost context
background.counter_bias: rejects a clean narrative when logs, timing, or independent evidence do not support it

readers.thesis: The conversation repeatedly converts trust from a provider promise into an inspectable chain of authority; CI ownership begins when every job identity, handoff, and destructive boundary is explicit and recoverable.
observations: The request rejects a self-hosted worker that still receives canonical job authority from an external provider.
The preceding repository-preview build demonstrates utility but remains commodity execution rather than Foundation build authority.
A disposable worker protects continuity only when job records and artifacts survive worker destruction.
uncertainties: The complete private chronology was unavailable, so earlier corrections and commitments outside the visible context were not reconstructed.
No live ephemeral virtual-machine runner executed a real repository build in this run.

background.discipline: distributed systems capacity engineering and infrastructure economics
background.experience: models queue saturation, noisy-neighbor failure, fleet replacement, cost cliffs, and operator load
background.attention_bias: maps control-plane authority, isolation boundaries, scaling bottlenecks, patch obligations, and exit costs
background.counter_bias: rejects technical sovereignty claims that conceal unsustainable staffing or idle-capacity costs

readers.thesis: An owned CI plane combines self-hosted job authority with disposable compute; sovereignty fails when runner reuse, mutable dependencies, provider-held logs, or unrehearsed recovery preserve hidden external authority.
observations: Forgejo supports server-controlled ephemeral runner registration, but the project still supplies the external orchestrator that creates and destroys machines.
Container isolation alone does not close the hostile-workflow boundary; a disposable VM or microVM contains the runner and its container engine.
GitHub self-hosted runners remain operationally useful during migration but retain GitHub as scheduler and impose service-driven runner update deadlines.
uncertainties: Cold-start time and cost for disposable VM versus microVM workers remain unbenchmarked.
The selected isolation layer requires a later hardware-virtualization compatibility test on actual project-owned or rented hosts.

agreements: Both readers reject persistent shared runners and host execution.
Both bind CI ownership to canonical job records, owned artifact custody, and clean control-plane restore.
Both preserve GitHub or other compute providers only as replaceable migration and capacity resources.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes traceable authorization and incident reconstruction; Reader B prioritizes isolation economics and fleet operability.
Reader A accepts disposable full virtual machines as the clearest initial boundary; Reader B keeps microVMs open as the likely scaling optimization after measurement.
omissions_detected: The artifact gate does not yet provision Forgejo, a runner orchestrator, or a disposable machine.
No benchmark measures cold start, cache behavior, bandwidth, concurrency, or complete control-plane recovery time.
Prior reader-pair rotation could not be exhaustively checked because the complete receipt catalog was unavailable.
revisions_after_discussion: The implementation adopts disposable virtual machines first and treats microVMs as a benchmarked substitution rather than an assumed optimization.
The recovery gate adds a second-operator drill so technical restoration does not conceal single-person dependency.

notes_added: Treat every CI runner identity as single-use and destroy its workspace, credentials, and machine boundary after one job.
Treat the CI scheduler, canonical job record, workflow source, and artifact destination as one authority chain that survives replacement of all worker compute.
notes_challenged: Owning a self-hosted runner means owning CI authority.
A fresh container alone provides a sufficient boundary for hostile build code.

privacy.review: Passed abstraction review: contains architecture, synthetic capability statements, reader backgrounds, coverage limits, and test results only; excludes transcript text, personal facts, health, animals, credentials, locations, deleted text, and uniquely identifying fragments.
